title: CertOps Agent Protocol Message
description: M4 agent/executor message envelope and per-messageType bodies under TOKENTIMER_CERTOPS_PLAN v1.19 sections 7.2-7.6 and ADR-0002/0003. The agent is outbound-only (it polls the control plane); the control plane never opens connections to agents. Messages MUST NOT carry private key material (enforced by apps/api/utils/secretMaterial.js at the ingest boundary). Bodies here are bootstrap-phase shapes for the packages/agent client stubs; server-side routes, Ed25519 signature verification, and replay-cache enforcement land with the Phase 4 control-plane agent backend (Dev A lane) and must stay in lockstep with this file.

protocolVersion.description: Agent protocol semver the agent speaks (7.6 version negotiation). The register message is the only messageType where agentId is a placeholder pending assignment.

messageType.description: register/heartbeat/claim/result/evidence. See definitions.*Body below for the per-messageType payload.

agentId.description: Stable agent identifier. For messageType=register this is a client-generated candidate id echoed back by the control plane; the control plane is the source of truth once registration succeeds (7.2).

clockOffsetMs.description: Agent-reported offset vs control-plane time, used for clock-drift detection (7.4/M4). Present on heartbeat and result; optional elsewhere.

body.description: Message-type-specific payload. Structurally validated against the matching *Body definition below via the if/then rules in `allOf`; each body definition is additionalProperties:false, so unconstrained extra fields are rejected per messageType.

registerBody.description: 7.2 agent identity lifecycle: POST /agents/register + single-use hashed bootstrap token. The raw bootstrap token travels as a bearer credential (outside this JSON body, per the machine-token pattern in 7.1), never as a body field; bootstrapTokenId is an opaque non-secret reference used for correlation/audit only.

declaredTargetSelectors.description: Non-secret target scope declared at registration (7.5 item 3, defense in depth: server must not offer jobs outside this declared scope).

declaredCommandProfileNames.description: Names only of agent-local command profiles (7.5): argv itself never leaves the agent config file.

heartbeatBody.description: 7.2/7.6 steady-state heartbeat: updates last_seen_at, carries version-negotiation and clock-drift-relevant fields. A retired agent's heartbeat returns 410 without updating last_seen_at (7.7 item 11); that behavior lives server-side and is out of scope for this body shape.

ntpSynced.description: Optional local NTP sync status, used alongside clockOffsetMs for fleet clock-drift alerts (M4 clock drift handling).

pinnedSigningKeyId.description: Job-signing public key id currently pinned by the agent (7.4), so the control plane can detect agents still on a retiring key during rotation overlap.

claimBody.description: 7.3 job dispatch model: the agent polls a claim endpoint (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED server-side); this body only expresses the agent's own poll preferences, never a specific job selection.

maxJobs.description: Maximum number of jobs the agent is willing to claim in this poll.

supportedActions.description: Subset of job-payload.schema.json actions this agent build can execute; lets the claim query skip jobs the agent cannot run.

resultBody.description: 7.4 job integrity and 7.7 result reporting. Distinct from the M2 external-executor events surface (executor-event.schema.json): this is the signed-job execution result path added in M4. attemptId is single-use per the server-side claim/lease model (7.4).

rejectionReason.description: Populated when status is rejected/blocked due to agent-local policy (7.5) or job-integrity/replay checks (7.4). Always reported as evidence too, per 7.5's 'operator sees policy conflicts, not silent failures' rule.

keyRotated.description: 9.3 key rotation policy: whether this job generated a new key vs reused the existing one. Never accompanied by the key itself.

evidenceBody.description: Agent-originated evidence, structurally aligned with evidence.schema.json's public fields but scoped to the message envelope's jobId/agentId rather than repeating them per item.
